answer:
All Waterfall.io API requests are authenticated by including an API key in the x-api-key HTTP header [1][2][3]. You must use the key provided in your contract to access the API [3][4][5].

```bash
x-api-key: <your-api-key>
```

For enterprise users, API key management allows the use of a master key to generate and manage sub-keys [6][3]. The master key is required to perform management operations (such as listing or creating keys), and requests made to these management endpoints must include the master key in the x-api-key header [6].

results.title: API Keys - List - Waterfall.io API Documentation
API Keys - Modify - Waterfall.io API Documentation
